evals.prompt: I'm building a hotel booking screen in my React Native app and I want users to pick a check-in and check-out date. Minimum stay is 2 nights, max is 14 nights, and they can't pick the same day for both. Use react-native-fast-calendar. The app is dark-themed (zinc-900 background, blue-400 accent). Show the number of nights once both dates are picked, and a Confirm/Clear footer. Hand me a single screen component called BookingDateScreen.tsx.
evals.expected_output: A single .tsx file using <Calendar.Root mode='range'> with minRangeDays=2, maxRangeDays=14, allowSameDay={false}, a custom dark theme that matches the brief, a header with month/year labels and chevrons (built with useCalendarHeader), a readout that computes nights from startDate/endDate, an action bar wired to useCalendarActions (clear/confirm/canConfirm). Should NOT use SimpleCalendar (custom footer needs the headless API).

evals.prompt: Add an iOS Calendar-style infinite scrolling calendar to my profile screen. Each row is a month; the user can scroll forwards or backwards forever. Some days are 'video upload days' and need to render a small thumbnail image instead of the day number. Tapping any day selects it (single mode). Include a 'Today' button that scrolls back to the current month and selects today's date. Hand me UploadScheduleCalendar.tsx — assume @legendapp/list is already installed.
evals.expected_output: A .tsx file with: a VerticalMonthList using LegendList from @legendapp/list with WINDOW_RADIUS/WINDOW_GROWTH pattern, onStartReached/onEndReached growth, maintainVisibleContentPosition, recycleItems, a stable EXTRA_DATA at module scope. A MonthSection wrapped in React.memo using the two-layer memo pattern (Layer 1 keyed on year+month for buildMonthGrid, Layer 2 keyed on selection state for cellInfos). renderDay (NOT components.DayCell) is used for the image cell so it's instance-local. Outside-month cells render as blank spacers. A Today button uses an imperative ref (scrollToMonth) — not just selectDate(today) — because displayed-watching effects don't fire when displayed already points at today's month.

evals.prompt: I want to show a date picker inside a bottom sheet when the user taps 'Schedule meeting'. Single date only, can't be in the past, max 90 days out. When the user confirms, dismiss the sheet and return the picked Date to the parent screen via an onSelect callback. Use @gorhom/bottom-sheet (already installed). Give me MeetingDatePickerSheet.tsx that exports a forwardRef component with present()/dismiss() methods, plus a small example of how to use it from a screen.
evals.expected_output: A .tsx file with a forwardRef component exposing present/dismiss via useImperativeHandle. Uses BottomSheetModal + BottomSheetView from @gorhom/bottom-sheet, BottomSheetBackdrop with pressBehavior='close'. Renders <Calendar.Root mode='single'> inside the sheet (NOT SimpleCalendar — the agent might need to know SimpleCalendar can't share its store with siblings). Has minDate=new Date(), maxDate=new Date(Date.now() + 90*86400000). The confirm action both fires onConfirm AND dismisses the sheet. Includes a brief usage snippet showing how the parent screen calls .present() and reads the picked Date via onSelect.
